title: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
authors: Zheng, Lianmin; Chiang, Wei-Lin; Sheng, Ying; Zhuang, Siyuan; Wu, Zhanghao; Zhuang, Yonghao; Lin, Zi; Li, Zhuohan; Li, Dacheng; Xing, Eric; Zhang, Hao; Gonzalez, Joseph; Stoica, Ion
description: Foundational paper proposing MT-Bench and the LLM-as-judge paradigm. Shows strong agreement with human preferences (>80%) for GPT-4 as judge, establishing the methodology used widely in subsequent work.

title: A Survey on LLM-as-a-Judge
authors: Li, Jiawei; Sun, Chengyu; Mao, Siqing; Hu, Ziniu; Li, Lei
description: Comprehensive survey covering strategies to enhance LLM-judge reliability, including bias mitigation, consistency improvements, and a novel benchmark for evaluating judge systems.

title: Justice or Prejudice? Quantifying Biases in LLM-as-a-Judge
authors: Huang, Jiayi; Li, Yanbo; Li, Yuning; Tang, Jie
description: Identifies 12 key biases in LLM judges and proposes the CALM framework for automated bias quantification. Examines fallacy-oversight, authority, sentiment, and diversity biases.

title: Judging the Judges: A Systematic Study of Position Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge
authors: Wang, Peiyi; Li, Lei; Chen, Liang; Cai, Feifan; Zhu, Dawei; Lin, Binghuai; Cao, Yunbo; Liu, Qi; Liu, Tianyu; Sui, Zhifang
description: Evaluates position bias across pairwise and list-wise settings. Introduces metrics for repetition stability, position consistency, and preference fairness.

title: LLMs-as-Judges: A Comprehensive Survey on LLM-based Evaluation Methods
authors: Li, Haitao; Chen, Qianqian; Zhang, Jiahui; Wang, Guochao; Li, Yiran
description: Broad survey of LLM-based evaluation covering scoring methods, design choices, and known limitations across multiple domains.

title: Self-Preference Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge
authors: Panickssery, Arjun; Bowman, Samuel; Feng, Shi
description: Demonstrates that LLM evaluators recognize and favor their own generations. Shows linear correlation between self-recognition capability and self-preference bias strength. Root cause identified as perplexity-based preference.

title: LLM Evaluators Recognize and Favor Their Own Generations
authors: Panickssery, Arjun; Bowman, Samuel; Feng, Shi
description: Earlier version showing GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet systematically assign higher scores to their own outputs and exhibit family-bias for models in the same family.

title: SLMQuant: Benchmarking Small Language Model Quantization for Practical Deployment
description: First systematic benchmark for LLM compression applied to small language models. Reveals fundamental disparities between SLMs and LLMs in quantization sensitivity through multi-track evaluations.

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Quantization Strategies for Large Language Models
description: Proposes three evaluation dimensions for quantization: knowledge and capacity, alignment, and efficiency. Shows 4-bit quantized LLMs can retain comparable performance to non-quantized counterparts.

title: Quantization Methods, Task Difficulty, and Model Size
description: Studies interaction between quantization method, task difficulty, and model size. Shows smaller models at higher bitwidths outperform larger models at extreme quantization.

title: Which Quantization Should I Use? A Unified Evaluation of llama.cpp Quantization on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
description: Comprehensive comparison of GGUF quantization formats specifically on Llama-3.1-8B using llama.cpp. Evaluates across GSM8K, HellaSwag, IFEval, MMLU, and TruthfulQA.

title: Small Language Models: Survey, Measurements, and Insights
authors: Lu, Zhenyan; Wang, Xiang; Guo, Bingning; Gao, Jiajun
description: Collects 57 popular SLMs and benchmarks capabilities including commonsense reasoning, math, in-context learning, and on-device runtime costs across quantization algorithms.

title: Benchmarking Post-Training Quantization in LLMs: Comprehensive Taxonomy, Unified Evaluation, and Comparative Analysis
description: Comprehensive taxonomy of post-training quantization methods with unified evaluation framework comparing GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF and other approaches.

title: Production-Grade Local LLM Inference on Apple Silicon: A Comparative Study of MLX, MLC-LLM, Ollama, llama.cpp, and PyTorch MPS
description: Compares five inference frameworks on Apple Silicon. MLX achieves ~230 tok/s, llama.cpp ~150 tok/s, Ollama 20-40 tok/s. Measures throughput, TTFT, and memory usage.

title: Benchmarking On-Device Machine Learning on Apple Silicon with MLX
description: Evaluates MLX framework for ML inference on Apple Silicon, measuring latency across transformer architectures and quantization schemes on M-series chips.

title: Native LLM and MLLM Inference at Scale on Apple Silicon
authors: Barrios, Wayner
description: Evaluation across M2 Ultra, M2 Max, M4 Pro for LLM and multimodal LLM inference. M4 Max achieves up to 525 tok/s. Covers quantization effects on Apple Silicon specifically.

title: Benchmarking and Characterization of Large Language Model Inference on Apple Silicon
description: Peer-reviewed characterization of LLM inference on Apple Silicon covering memory bandwidth, GPU utilization, and scaling behavior across model sizes.

title: Exploring the Impact of Temperature on Large Language Models: Hot or Cold?
description: Studies temperature effects across small (1-4B), medium (6-13B), and large (40-80B) models. Introduces mutation temperature concept — the threshold at which performance degrades. Larger models more robust to temperature increases.

title: The Good, The Bad, and The Greedy: Evaluation of LLMs Should Not Ignore Non-Determinism
authors: Renze, Matthew; Guven, Erhan
description: Argues LLM benchmarks using only greedy decoding miss important variance information. Shows sampling-based evaluation reveals significant performance differences between models not visible under deterministic decoding.

title: Verbosity Does Not Equal Veracity: Demystify Verbosity Compensation Behavior of Large Language Models
description: Demonstrates strong correlation between conciseness and correctness. Verbose responses correlate with high uncertainty and lower accuracy. Models compensate for low confidence with extra text.

title: Verbosity Bias in Preference Labeling by Large Language Models
authors: Saito, Keita
description: Shows both LLM and human annotators prefer longer responses even when content quality is equal, encoding verbosity bias into reward models during RLHF.

title: Disentangling Length from Quality in Direct Preference Optimization
description: Proposes methods to separate length from quality signals in preference optimization. Shows that DPO without correction systematically favors longer outputs.

title: Explaining Length Bias in LLM-Based Preference Evaluations
description: Finds positive correlation between response length and win rate in LLM evaluations. Excessive length inflates perceived quality through increased information mass.

title: Exploring Temperature Effects on Large Language Models Across Various Clinical Tasks
description: Tests GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Llama-3-70b at five temperature settings on clinical tasks. GPT-4 and Llama-3 show remarkable consistency regardless of temperature, suggesting large models are robust.

title: Sample Smart, Not Hard: Correctness-First Decoding for Better Reasoning in LLMs
description: Proposes correctness-first decoding combining stochastic sampling with verification. Self-consistency with controlled sampling (t=0.8, top_p=0.9) outperforms greedy decoding on math reasoning.

title: Open-weight models lag state-of-the-art by around 3 months on average
description: Analysis showing the open-weight frontier trails closed models by approximately 3 months on average, with the gap varying over time and sometimes closing entirely.

title: Frontier AI capabilities can be run at home within a year or less
description: Shows frontier capabilities become available on consumer hardware within approximately one year, driven by model distillation, quantization, and hardware improvements.

title: We ran over half a million evaluations on quantized LLMs — here's what we found
description: Largest-scale quantization evaluation study. Tests across academic benchmarks, real-world tasks, and manual inspection. Shows 8B models experience slight variability when quantized but preserve core semantic meaning.

title: Quantized Transformer Language Model Implementations on Edge Devices
description: Demonstrates quantized MobileBERT achieves 160x smaller footprint vs BERT-large with only 4.1% accuracy drop on edge devices.

title: Evaluating quantized Large Language Models for code generation on low-resource language benchmarks
description: Shows quantized 4-bit and 8-bit 7B models can run on laptops without dedicated GPU for practical code generation tasks.

title: How Reliable is Multilingual LLM-as-a-Judge?
description: Examines inconsistency of LLM-as-judge across languages. Shows failure to maintain evaluation consistency in multilingual settings.

title: An Empirical Study of LLM-as-a-Judge: How Design Choices Impact Evaluation Reliability
description: Studies how reference answers, score descriptions, sampling strategy, and CoT affect judge reliability. Non-deterministic sampling improves alignment with human preferences.

title: The Effect of Sampling Temperature on Problem Solving in Large Language Models
authors: Renze, Matthew; Guven, Erhan
description: Empirical study showing temperature changes from 0.0 to 1.0 do not have statistically significant impact on LLM problem-solving performance. Results generalize across LLMs, prompt techniques, and problem domains.

title: Is Temperature the Creativity Parameter of Large Language Models?
authors: Peeperkorn, Max; Kouwenhoven, Tom; Brown, Dan;"; Jordanous, Anna
description: Finds temperature is weakly correlated with novelty and moderately correlated with incoherence, but has no relationship with cohesion or typicality in creative generation tasks.

title: The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration
authors: Holtzman, Ari; Buys, Jan; Du, Li; Forbes, Maxwell; Choi, Yejin
description: Foundational paper proposing nucleus (top-p) sampling. Shows maximization-based decoding produces degenerate text and that truncating the unreliable probability tail via dynamic nucleus yields high-quality diverse text.

title: Benchmark Squared: Systematic Evaluation of LLM Benchmarks
authors: Qian, Qi; Huang, Chengsong; Xu, Jingwen; Lv, Changze; Wu, Muling; Liu, Wenhao; Wang, Xiaohua; Zheng, Xiaoqing
description: Meta-evaluation framework for LLM benchmarks using cross-benchmark ranking consistency, discriminability score, and capability alignment deviation. Evaluated 15 benchmarks across 11 LLMs.

title: On Robustness and Reliability of Benchmark-Based Evaluation of LLMs
description: Assesses LLM robustness by paraphrasing benchmark questions and evaluates whether model rankings remain stable across input variations.

title: Understanding Large Language Models in Your Pockets: Performance Study on COTS Mobile Devices
description: Comprehensive evaluation of LLM inference on mobile platforms including throughput, latency, memory usage, and power consumption across different model sizes and quantization levels.

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Quantized Instruction-Tuned Large Language Models: An Experimental Analysis up to 405B
description: Large-scale analysis of quantized instruction-tuned models from 7B to 405B. Shows quantization impact varies by model size, with smaller models being more sensitive.

title: Improving Code Generation by Dynamic Temperature Sampling
authors: Zhu, Yuqi; Li, Jia
description: Proposes dynamic temperature guided by KL-divergence that outperforms fixed-temperature top-k and top-p sampling on conversational QA and summarization tasks.

title: Contextual Temperature for Language Modeling
authors: Wang, Pei-Hsin; Hsieh, Cho-Jui
description: Proposes learned contextual temperature that adapts per-token based on context, improving generation quality over fixed temperature approaches.

title: Pitfalls of Evaluating Language Models with Open Benchmarks
description: Demonstrates vulnerability of open benchmarks like HELM to gaming. Shows even small cheating models can achieve high scores through memorization rather than true capability.

title: Mitigating Length Bias in RLHF through a Causal Lens
description: Proposes causal framework for understanding and mitigating length bias in RLHF-trained models. Shows length bias arises from confounded reward signals.

title: Adaptive Length Bias Mitigation in Reward Models for RLHF
description: Develops adaptive methods for mitigating length bias in reward models used for RLHF training, enabling quality-focused rather than length-focused optimization.

title: Gemma 4 Model Card
authors: Google DeepMind
description: Detailed specifications for the Gemma 4 family (E2B, E4B, 26B, 31B) including the Per-Layer Embedding architecture for edge efficiency.

title: LLM-as-a-Judge: automated evaluation of search query parsing using large language models
authors: Amadou, Ibrahim; Charradi, Abderrahmane
description: Validates LLM-driven automated evaluation for structured tasks and achieves over 90 percent alignment with human judgments.

title: JudgeLM: Fine-tuned Large Language Models are Scalable Judges
authors: Zhu, Dawei; Zhang, Canbin; Wang, Boxuan
description: Proposes a family of models specifically fine-tuned to act as judges for open-ended and structured LLM outputs.

title: Diverging Preferences: When do Annotators Disagree and do Models Know?
authors: Sorensen, Taylor; Moore, Caleb
description: Investigates biases in LLM-as-judge systems, finding they favor specific response strategies like overton responses over clarifying questions.

title: Ollama
description: Local LLM runner that transitioned to the Apple MLX backend in version 0.19 to optimize performance on Apple Silicon hardware.

title: llama.cpp
authors: Gerganov, Georgi
description: Foundational inference engine supporting GGUF quantization and native acceleration for a wide range of consumer hardware.

title: MLX
description: High-performance machine learning framework built natively for Apple Silicon to leverage unified memory architecture.

title: LM Studio
description: Desktop application for running and benchmarking local LLMs with support for advanced quantization formats and hardware acceleration.

title: GLM-4-9B: Specifications and GPU VRAM Requirements
description: Technical specification of the 9B GLM-4 model detailing its GQA architecture and optimal quantization levels for local deployment.

title: The Role of Temperature and Top-p in LLM Accuracy and Creativity
authors: Lundgren, Johannes
description: Analyzes how sampling parameters affect the balance between factual precision and creative variability in small language models.

title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
authors: DeepSeek-AI
description: Demonstrates the emergence of complex reasoning capabilities in open-weight models through pure reinforcement learning.

title: We Tested Quantized LLMs: Cost and Performance Results
authors: Badaoui, Madani
description: Empirical benchmark of 4-bit and 8-bit quantized models on Apple M-series chips identifying the 2-bit quality cliff.

title: A survey on evaluation of large language models
authors: Chang, Yupeng; Wang, Xu; Wang, Jindong
source: ACM Transactions on Intelligent Systems and Technology
description: Comprehensive review of LLM evaluation metrics and biases including the correlation between response verbosity and judge preference.

title: Jan
description: Open-source offline ChatGPT alternative that supports GGUF and TensorRT backends for private local inference.

title: Faraday
description: Native desktop application for managing local LLMs with a focus on ease of use for Apple Silicon and consumer GPU users.

title: Meta
description: Developer of the Llama model series which serves as the foundational architecture for the majority of quantized small LLM research.

title: Google DeepMind
description: Research organization behind the Gemma series of open-weight models optimized for on-device and edge deployment.

title: Zhipu AI
description: Leading developer of the GLM model family which provides high-performance bilingual alternatives in the 7-9B parameter space.

title: GGUF Format Specification
description: Technical documentation for the standard binary format used to distribute quantized models for local inference runners.

title: Q8BERT: Quantized 8Bit BERT
authors: Zafrir, Ofir; Boudoukh, Guy; Izsak, Peter; Wasserblat, Moshe
description: Foundational pre-LLM quantization paper showing that aggressive post-training quantization can preserve NLP quality while reducing inference cost, useful as an adjacent precursor to LLM PTQ work.

title: On Decoding Strategies for Neural Text Generators
authors: Wiher, Gian; Meister, Clara; Cotterell, Ryan
source: Transactions of the Association for Computational Linguistics
description: Empirically compares decoding strategies across tasks and shows that decoding effects are task-dependent rather than universally optimal.

title: Truncation Sampling as Language Model Desmoothing
authors: Hewitt, John; Manning, Christopher D.; Liang, Percy
source: Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: EMNLP 2022
description: Analyzes top-k and top-p as desmoothing methods and proposes eta-sampling, making it highly relevant to temperature and nucleus-sampling studies.

title: Turning Up the Heat: Min-p Sampling for Creative and Coherent LLM Outputs
authors: Minh, Nguyen Nhat; Baker, Andrew; Neo, Clement; Roush, Allen; Kirsch, Andreas; Shwartz-Ziv, Ravid
description: Introduces min-p sampling and benchmarks it against standard truncation strategies, offering a useful adjacent reference for decoding quality tradeoffs.

title: LLM.int8(): 8-bit Matrix Multiplication for Transformers at Scale
authors: Dettmers, Tim; Lewis, Mike; Belkada, Younes; Zettlemoyer, Luke
description: Foundational 8-bit inference method for transformer models and a key reference for memory-quality tradeoffs in local LLM deployment.

title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers
authors: Frantar, Elias; Ashkboos, Saleh; Hoefler, Torsten; Alistarh, Dan
description: Landmark paper on accurate post-training quantization for generative transformers and central to GPTQ-based local inference stacks.

title: SmoothQuant: Accurate and Efficient Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models
authors: Xiao, Guangxuan; Lin, Ji; Seznec, Mickael; Wu, Hao; Demouth, Julien; Han, Song
source: Proceedings of the 40th International Conference on Machine Learning
description: Widely cited PTQ method for LLMs that shifts quantization difficulty between weights and activations to improve low-bit inference quality.

title: AWQ: Activation-aware Weight Quantization for LLM Compression and Acceleration
authors: Lin, Ji; Tang, Jiaming; Tang, Haotian; Yang, Shang; Chen, Wei-Ming
description: Highly relevant 4-bit quantization method that is frequently used in practice for efficient local inference on consumer hardware.

title: A Survey of Low-bit Large Language Models: Basics, Systems, and Algorithms
authors: Gong, Ruihao; Ding, Yifu; Wang, Zining; Lv, Chengtao; Zheng, Xingyu
description: Comprehensive survey of low-bit LLM methods spanning quantization algorithms, systems support, and deployment tradeoffs.

title: RooflineBench: A Benchmarking Framework for On-Device LLMs via Roofline Analysis
authors: Bi, Zhen; Chen, Xueshu; Sun, Luoyang; Yao, Yuhang; Shen, Qing
description: Proposes a benchmarking framework for on-device LLMs that helps explain quality-latency-memory tradeoffs from a systems perspective.

title: On-device Llama 3.1 with Core ML
description: Official Apple technical write-up on running Llama 3.1 locally with Core ML on Apple Silicon.

title: CRAG - Comprehensive RAG Benchmark
authors: Yang, Xiao; Sun, Kai; Xin, Hao; Sun, Yushi; Bhalla, Nikita
description: A major benchmark for retrieval-augmented and context-grounded QA, useful for testing whether smaller local models stay faithful to provided evidence.

title: GaRAGe: A Benchmark with Grounding Annotations for RAG Evaluation
authors: Sorodoc, Ionut Teodor; Ribeiro, Leonardo F. R.; Blloshmi, Rexhina; Davis, Christopher; de Gispert, Adria
description: Provides grounding annotations for retrieval-augmented generation evaluation, useful for studying factual faithfulness.

title: Large Language Models are not Fair Evaluators
authors: Wang, Peiyi; Li, Lei; Chen, Liang; Cai, Zefan; Zhu, Dawei
description: Shows that LLM judges exhibit systematic unfairness and bias, including sensitivity to superficial response properties and ordering effects.

title: Judge Reliability Harness: Stress Testing the Reliability of LLM Judges
authors: Dev, Sunishchal; Sloan, Andrew; Kavner, Joshua; Kong, Nicholas; Sandler, Morgan
description: Introduces a stress-testing harness for LLM judges probing robustness to paraphrase, formatting, verbosity, and stochastic variation.
